pattern: Counting subsequences that avoid three same-parity elements in a row is a small-state DP that tracks, per parity, how many subsequences currently end in a run of one versus two of that parity.
hints: Only the parity of each element matters, and only the tail of the chosen subsequence: how long the current same-parity run is.
Keep four running counts: subsequences ending in a run of one even, two evens, one odd, two odds. A new element can extend or restart a run but never make it length three.

When you see an even value, the new single-even count becomes (everything that ended odd, plus one fresh start), and the double-even count becomes the old single-even count; carry odds unchanged, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: three odds, the full triple is unstable
long even run, many unstable subsequences excluded
alternating parities never form a same-parity triple

stress.description: random parities to stress the four-state recurrence and the modulus
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
